particle_size_range: Micro- and nanoplastics (MNPs); specific size ranges not quantified in provided context
key_metrics_output: Risk assessment framework evaluation; hazard identification; exposure assessment; risk characterization; toxicologically relevant metrics; weight of evidence approach
abstract: This perspective evaluates six established human health risk assessment frameworks (RAFs) for micro- and nanoplastics (MNPs) to assess their technological and regulatory readiness. The authors propose nine technical criteria that RAFs should meet to inform policy, including quantifiability of outputs, systematic evaluation of input data quality, consistency between exposure and effect data, addressing complexity and diversity of environmentally realistic microplastics, readiness for integration into existing regulatory approaches, and real-world implementation status. The paper discusses strengths of each framework and recommends combining them into a single overarching framework that integrates these strengths to support comprehensive human health risk assessment and governance of MNPs.
